articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in Summer 2027
articles.summary: A Hasbro-branded family entertainment center is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee in summer 2027, bringing major IP licensing into the FEC space. This signals a growing trend of toy and entertainment brands entering the location-based entertainment market. FEC operators and investors should watch how branded IP partnerships shape competitive positioning in family leisure destinations.

articles.title: Embed Integrates with BMI Merchandise for Streamlined FEC Redemption Management
articles.summary: Embed has added BMI Merchandise's RFID-based redemption inventory system to its ecosystem integrations, joining existing partnerships with Square POS and QubicaAMF. The integration allows bowling and FEC operators to manage prize redemption inventory centrally with minimal manual labor, verifying hundreds of SKUs in seconds. This is a practical operational upgrade for multi-attraction venues looking to reduce labor costs and improve inventory accuracy.

articles.title: Intercard Cashless Technology Installed in 23rd SpeedPark FEC Location in France
articles.summary: Intercard has completed its 23rd cashless technology installation for French amusement operator SpeedPark, this time at a new 54,000-square-foot FEC in Rennes featuring go-karts, bowling, laser tag, and arcades. The continued rollout highlights the growing adoption of cashless payment systems across multi-attraction FECs in Europe. For operators evaluating cashless infrastructure, this scale of deployment with a single vendor demonstrates long-term reliability and scalability.

articles.title: AAMA Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson Resigns After 10 Years
articles.summary: The American Amusement Machine Association has announced that Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson has resigned effective June 28, 2026, following a decade in the role. Gustafson has been a key figure in the coin-op and amusement machine industry, and his departure marks a significant leadership transition for the trade group. FEC operators and suppliers who rely on AAMA for advocacy and industry standards will want to monitor who is named to fill the role.

articles.title: Little Lion Entertainment to Open Large-Scale Arcade Arena on London's Southbank
articles.summary: Little Lion Entertainment will launch Arcade Arena London in September at a purpose-built venue on the Southbank, featuring Chaos Karts, an immersive Pac-Man experience developed with Bandai Namco, and a new laser tag and drone experience called Alien Invasion. The venue is designed as a large-format live gaming destination hosting multiple world-class experiences simultaneously. This represents a notable escalation in the scale and IP sophistication of competitive socializing and immersive arcade venues.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens New Location at Potomac Mills in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park has opened a new venue at the Potomac Mills mall in Northern Virginia, expanding the indoor slide park concept into another major market. The brand's continued retail-adjacent expansion strategy is relevant for FEC operators and mall landlords evaluating anchor tenant opportunities. Indoor action parks in mall settings continue to demonstrate viability as destination entertainment draws.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Opens in Atlanta Claiming Unique Concept for the Region
articles.summary: A new adventure park described as unlike any other in the country has opened in Atlanta, adding to the growing density of experiential entertainment options in the Southeast. The opening reflects continued investor and operator appetite for differentiated indoor attraction concepts in major metros. FEC operators in the region should take note of the competitive landscape shift.

articles.title: Thrillz Atlanta Indoor Theme Park: Key Takeaways for FEC Operators
articles.summary: A breakdown of the Thrillz Atlanta indoor theme park concept offers FEC operators insight into how large-format indoor parks are positioning themselves in the market. The analysis covers attractions mix, target demographics, and operational considerations relevant to anyone planning or expanding an indoor entertainment venue. This type of competitive intelligence is valuable for operators benchmarking their own offerings.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's family entertainment center is investing in a multi-level go-kart attraction, a premium upgrade that signals continued capital investment in the go-kart segment of the FEC industry. Multi-level tracks are increasingly seen as differentiators that drive repeat visitation and justify premium pricing. This expansion is a useful case study for operators considering go-kart upgrades.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Releases 29-Country Research on Birthday Party Guest List Trends
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has published new consumer research spanning 29 countries examining how parents approach birthday party planning, including whether they limit guest lists. The data provides actionable intelligence for FEC operators whose birthday party programs are a core revenue driver. Understanding shifting parental preferences globally can help operators tailor party packages and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Interstellar Arc at AREA15 Wins Best Location-Based Entertainment at Auggie Awards
articles.summary: Interstellar Arc, an immersive XR experience at Las Vegas entertainment complex AREA15, won Best Location-Based Entertainment at the 17th annual Auggie Awards recognizing the best in augmented and extended reality. The recognition underscores the rising quality bar for location-based XR experiences and the competitive pressure on FEC operators to incorporate cutting-edge immersive technology. Operators and suppliers tracking the LBE technology landscape should note the experience as a benchmark.

articles.title: Chance Rides Emphasizes Long-Term Service and Parts Support as Key to Operator ROI
articles.summary: Ride manufacturer Chance Rides is highlighting how post-installation support, parts availability, and technical service are critical to maximizing the return on investment for amusement ride owners. The company argues that a ride's true success is measured over years of operation, not just at opening day. For FEC operators evaluating ride purchases, this perspective reinforces the importance of factoring in lifetime service capabilities alongside upfront acquisition cost.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. Plans New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. has announced plans to open a new indoor entertainment venue in Keller, Texas, adding to the growing number of independent operators launching creative FEC concepts in suburban markets. The Texas market continues to attract entertainment investment driven by strong population growth and family demographics. Operators and investors tracking greenfield development opportunities will find this opening noteworthy.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park Announces First Pennsylvania Location
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park has announced it will open its first Pennsylvania location next year, continuing the brand's geographic expansion across the U.S. The adventure park format, featuring climbing, jumping, and play-based attractions, remains one of the fastest-growing FEC sub-categories. Operators and landlords in the mid-Atlantic region should monitor this opening as a competitive development.

articles.title: High Tide VR Opens Near Bethany Beach, Offering Immersive Entertainment Alternative
articles.summary: High Tide VR, a new VR-focused entertainment venue, has opened near Bethany Beach, Delaware, offering visitors an immersive technology-based alternative to traditional beach-area amusements. The venue highlights the continued proliferation of VR entertainment concepts in secondary and seasonal markets. FEC operators considering VR as an attraction add-on will find the stand-alone venue model a useful reference point.

articles.title: Seabreeze Amusement Park Opens Reef Runners, Largest Single Investment in Park's History
articles.summary: Seabreeze Amusement Park in Rochester, New York has opened Reef Runners, a 50-foot-tall water slide complex stretching over 1,000 feet, representing the largest single capital investment in the park's 146-year history and the first of its kind in the Northeast. The project demonstrates how legacy parks are making bold capital commitments to drive differentiation and visitation. For FEC operators and investors, this is a strong example of strategic reinvestment in physical attractions to maintain competitive relevance.

articles.title: Drone Light Shows Emerge as a Sustainable Alternative to Fireworks for Attraction Closings
articles.summary: A detailed industry analysis examines the growing shift from traditional fireworks to drone light shows as the nightly spectacle closing for amusement parks and attractions. Drone shows offer advantages including noise reduction, no fire risk, reusability, and appeal to noise-sensitive guests and neighbors. FEC operators who currently use fireworks or are planning evening entertainment programs should consider the operational and community relations benefits of this technology.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New FEC Destination in Greater Noida West, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has launched a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, India, reflecting robust growth in the FEC sector across South Asian urban markets. The opening highlights international expansion opportunities as middle-class consumer spending on experiential entertainment rises in emerging markets. Suppliers and franchise operators with global ambitions should track this growth corridor.

articles.title: Katapult Delivers Turnkey Interactive Lego Ninjago Pop-Up Exhibitions Across Global Legoland Parks
articles.summary: Experience design agency Katapult partnered with The Lego Group and Merlin Entertainments to create interactive Ninjago anniversary exhibitions deployed simultaneously at Legoland parks in the UK, Germany, Korea, and China. Each installation was custom-designed for its venue while maintaining brand consistency, showcasing how major operators manage IP-driven temporary attractions at scale. FEC operators considering pop-up or rotating attractions as a visitation driver can draw lessons from this approach.

articles.title: DreamLab Immersive Positions AI and Interactive Storytelling as the Next Frontier for Guest Experiences
articles.summary: DreamLab Immersive, rebranded from DE-ZYN Studios, is expanding its focus on AI-integrated storytelling and media design for theme parks and location-based entertainment. Led by industry veteran John Miceli, the studio is pursuing projects that blend interactivity, emerging technology, and narrative for memorable guest experiences. FEC operators investing in immersive attraction upgrades should be aware of studios like DreamLab as potential design and technology partners.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth Closes After Three Decades
articles.summary: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center has announced it will shut down after approximately 30 years of operation, marking the end of a long-running independent FEC in a competitive Texas market. The closure illustrates the ongoing challenges facing legacy independent FEC operators competing against newer, larger multi-attraction venues. Industry observers should note the closure as a signal of the market pressures on older, single-format amusement centers.

articles.title: Dollywood Named to Newsweek's Top Workplaces 2026 Lists for U.S. and Tennessee
articles.summary: Dollywood theme park has been recognized on Newsweek's Greatest Workplaces 2026 lists for both the United States and the state of Tennessee, based on independent survey results. Workplace reputation is increasingly important for attraction operators competing for seasonal and full-time labor in a tight hospitality job market. FEC operators can look to Dollywood's employee culture initiatives as a benchmark for improving retention and recruitment.

articles.title: UK Price Cuts on Family Days Out Take Effect, Pressuring Attraction Pricing Strategies
articles.summary: New price reductions on family leisure and days-out activities have come into force in the UK, reflecting both consumer demand for affordability and competitive pressure across the attractions sector. For FEC operators in the UK and those watching international pricing trends, this development raises questions about value perception and dynamic pricing strategies. Operators should evaluate whether their current pricing structures align with shifting consumer expectations around family entertainment costs.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois this July, adding a fresh FEC option to the central Illinois market. While details on the specific attraction mix are limited, the opening reflects continued operator confidence in mid-size U.S. markets for family entertainment investment. Local operators and regional suppliers should monitor the concept for competitive and partnership opportunities.

articles.title: European Heatwave Forces Temporary Closure of Major Visitor Attractions
articles.summary: Record-breaking heat across the UK and France has forced major visitor attractions including the Louvre, Eiffel Tower, and Tower Bridge to close temporarily or reduce operating hours. The extreme weather event highlights the operational vulnerability of attractions — including those without full climate control — to climate-driven disruptions. FEC operators should review their heat management protocols and contingency plans as extreme weather events become more frequent.
